labs.frequency: daily glycemic monitoring in patients with advanced CKD
labs.clinicalContext: High hypoglycemia risk in patients with advanced CKD due to drugs that raise blood insulin levels

labs.frequency: periodic monitoring based on CKD stage
labs.actionRequired: avoid long-acting or renally cleared sulfonylureas
labs.clinicalContext: Long-acting or kidney-cleared sulfonylureas should be avoided at low eGFRs

targetRange.populationContext: patients with low GFR treated with sulfonylureas

labs.clinicalContext: Higher HbA1c targets may be selected for patients at higher risk for hypoglycemia

labRequirements.specialPopulationNotes: High hypoglycemia risk in patients with advanced CKD. Long-acting or kidney-cleared sulfonylureas should be avoided at low eGFRs. Higher HbA1c targets may be appropriate for patients with low GFR.
labRequirements.sourceSnippet: Medications associated with hypoglycemia: insulin, sulfonylureas, meglitinides. High hypoglycemia risk agents: insulin, sulfonylureas, meglitinides (raise blood insulin levels). Sulfonylureas that are long-acting or cleared by the kidney should be avoided at low eGFRs.
